---
name: classey-database
description: >
  Classey Appwrite database skill. Load this skill when working with ANY
  Appwrite collection, database query, data fetching hook, Zustand store
  with persisted data, TypeScript interface for database models, Zod schema
  for validation, or any feature that reads/writes to the database.
  Triggers: 'appwrite', 'collection', 'database', 'query', 'fetch', 'create',
  'update', 'delete', 'schema', 'interface', 'type', 'model', 'hook',
  'TanStack', 'useQuery', 'useMutation', 'semester', 'subject', 'attendance',
  'exam', 'task', 'event', 'slot', 'settings', 'grade', 'file', 'notification',
  'telegram', 'AI chat', 'backup', 'tag', 'goal', 'note', 'widget'.
---

# Classey Database — All 23 Appwrite Collections

NEVER guess a field name. NEVER invent a collection name. ALWAYS reference this
skill before writing any Appwrite query, TypeScript interface, or Zod schema.

---

## Appwrite Setup

```typescript
// src/lib/appwrite.ts
import { Client, Databases, Storage, Account, Messaging } from 'appwrite'

const client = new Client()
  .setEndpoint(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APPWRITE_ENDPOINT!)
  .setProject(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APPWRITE_PROJECT_ID!)

export const databases = new Databases(client)
export const storage = new Storage(client)
export const account = new Account(client)
export const messaging = new Messaging(client)

// Database ID — single database for entire app
export const DB_ID = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APPWRITE_DATABASE_ID!

// Collection IDs — all stored in env vars
export const COLLECTIONS = {
  SETTINGS:           process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_SETTINGS!,
  GRADE_SCALES:       process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_GRADE_SCALES!,
  SLOTS:              process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_SLOTS!,
  SEMESTERS:          process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_SEMESTERS!,
  SUBJECTS:           process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_SUBJECTS!,
  CLASS_SCHEDULES:    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_CLASS_SCHEDULES!,
  CLASS_OCCURRENCES:  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_CLASS_OCCURRENCES!,
  EXAMS:              process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_EXAMS!,
  TASKS:              process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_TASKS!,
  EVENTS:             process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_EVENTS!,
  HOLIDAYS:           process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_HOLIDAYS!,
  RESOURCE_LINKS:     process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_RESOURCE_LINKS!,
  FILES:              process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_FILES!,
  NOTIFICATIONS_LOG:  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_NOTIFICATIONS_LOG!,
  AUDIT_LOG:          process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_AUDIT_LOG!,
  TAGS:               process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_TAGS!,
  TAGGED_ITEMS:       process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_TAGGED_ITEMS!,
  STUDY_SESSIONS:     process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_STUDY_SESSIONS!,
  GOALS:              process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_GOALS!,
  NOTES:              process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_NOTES!,
  BACKUPS:            process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_BACKUPS!,
  WIDGETS_CONFIG:     process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_WIDGETS_CONFIG!,
  AI_CONVERSATIONS:   process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COL_AI_CONVERSATIONS!,
} as const

// Storage Bucket ID
export const BUCKET_ID = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APPWRITE_BUCKET_ID!
```

---

## TypeScript Interfaces + Zod Schemas

All interfaces extend AppwriteDocument for the base Appwrite fields.

```typescript
// src/types/database.ts

// Base Appwrite document fields (auto-generated by Appwrite)
interface AppwriteDocument {
  $id: string
  $createdAt: string  // ISO string
  $updatedAt: string  // ISO string
  $collectionId: string
  $databaseId: string
  $permissions: string[]
}
```

---

## Collection 1: `settings`

One document per user. Single-user app — always query by fixed user_id or
just get the first (and only) document.

```typescript
interface Settings extends AppwriteDocument {
  user_id: string

  // APPEARANCE
  theme_mode: 'dark' | 'light'
  background_style: 'spooky-smoke' | 'dotted' | 'boxes' | 'dot-pattern' | 'noise-grid'
  background_custom_css: string | null
  font_family: 'Nunito' | 'Poppins' | 'Quicksand' | string
  accent_color_default: string  // hex e.g. "#8B5CF6" (used when no active semester)

  // BEHAVIOR
  timezone: string              // default: "Asia/Kolkata"
  first_day_of_week: 0 | 1    // 0=Sunday, 1=Monday (default: 1)
  time_format: '12h' | '24h'  // default: '24h'
  date_format: 'DD/MM/YYYY' | 'MM/DD/YYYY' | 'YYYY-MM-DD'

  // GRADING
  spi_scale: number             // default: 10
  credits_range_max: number     // default: 100

  // ATTENDANCE
  auto_absent_hours: number     // default: 48
  default_attendance_requirement: number  // default: 75 (percent)

  // NOTIFICATIONS — PUSH (web push via Appwrite Messaging)
  push_notifications_enabled: boolean   // default: false
  push_notify_exams: boolean            // default: false
  push_notify_assignments: boolean      // default: false
  push_notify_deadlines: boolean        // default: false
  push_notify_tasks: boolean            // default: false

  // NOTIFICATIONS — TELEGRAM
  telegram_notifications_enabled: boolean // default: true
  telegram_bot_chat_id: string | null     // user's Telegram chat ID
  telegram_notify_exams: boolean          // default: true
  telegram_notify_assignments: boolean    // default: true
  telegram_notify_deadlines: boolean      // default: true
  telegram_notify_tasks: boolean          // default: true
  telegram_notify_classes: boolean        // default: false (master toggle)
  pre_class_reminder_minutes: number      // default: 15

  // AI
  ai_requests_today: number       // counter, resets at midnight IST
  ai_requests_reset_date: string  // ISO date string of last reset

  // MISC
  last_opened_path: string | null
}

const settingsSchema = z.object({
  theme_mode: z.enum(['dark', 'light']).default('dark'),
  background_style: z.enum(['spooky-smoke', 'dotted', 'boxes', 'dot-pattern',
    'noise-grid']).default('spooky-smoke'),
  font_family: z.string().default('Nunito'),
  accent_color_default: z.string().regex(/^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$/).default('#8B5CF6'),
  timezone: z.string().default('Asia/Kolkata'),
  first_day_of_week: z.union([z.literal(0), z.literal(1)]).default(1),
  time_format: z.enum(['12h', '24h']).default('24h'),
  date_format: z.enum(['DD/MM/YYYY', 'MM/DD/YYYY', 'YYYY-MM-DD'])
    .default('DD/MM/YYYY'),
  spi_scale: z.number().positive().default(10),
  auto_absent_hours: z.number().positive().default(48),
  default_attendance_requirement: z.number().min(0).max(100).default(75),
  pre_class_reminder_minutes: z.number().positive().default(15),
})
```

---

## Collection 2: `grade_scales`

Maps letter grades to point values. Default scale is pre-seeded on first run.

```typescript
interface GradeMapping {
  grade: string     // "A", "A-", "B+", "B", etc.
  points: number    // 10, 9, 8, 7, etc.
  min_percent: number  // minimum % to get this grade
}

interface GradeScale extends AppwriteDocument {
  name: string              // "Default Scale", "Custom Scale 1"
  is_default: boolean       // true for the system default
  mappings: string          // JSON stringified GradeMapping[] (Appwrite stores as string)
  // Parsed: GradeMapping[]
}

// DEFAULT SCALE (pre-seed on app init)
const DEFAULT_GRADE_MAPPINGS: GradeMapping[] = [
  { grade: 'A',  points: 10, min_percent: 90 },
  { grade: 'A-', points: 9,  min_percent: 85 },
  { grade: 'B+', points: 8,  min_percent: 80 },
  { grade: 'B',  points: 7,  min_percent: 75 },
  { grade: 'B-', points: 6,  min_percent: 70 },
  { grade: 'C+', points: 5,  min_percent: 65 },
  { grade: 'C',  points: 4,  min_percent: 60 },
  { grade: 'D',  points: 3,  min_percent: 50 },
  { grade: 'F',  points: 0,  min_percent: 0  },
]

// Helper to parse mappings
function parseGradeMappings(scale: GradeScale): GradeMapping[] {
  return JSON.parse(scale.mappings) as GradeMapping[]
}
```

---

## Collection 3: `slots`

College timetable slot system. Pre-seeded with default slots on init.
Users can add/edit/delete slots in settings.

```typescript
interface SubSlot {
  id: string          // "1A", "1B", "1C", "L1", "X1", etc.
  day_of_week: 1|2|3|4|5|6  // 1=Mon, 2=Tue, 3=Wed, 4=Thu, 5=Fri, 6=Sat
  start_time: string  // "08:30"
  end_time: string    // "09:25"
}

interface Slot extends AppwriteDocument {
  slot_id: string     // "1", "2", "L1", "X1" — user-facing ID
  name: string        // "Slot 1", "Lab Slot 1", "Wednesday Extra 1"
  type: 'regular' | 'lab' | 'extra'
  sub_slots: string   // JSON stringified SubSlot[]
  is_default: boolean // true for pre-seeded slots
  sort_order: number
  deleted_at: string | null
}

// PRE-SEEDED DEFAULT SLOTS
// REGULAR SLOTS (1-4): 3 sub-slots each, ~55 min
// Slot 1: 1A Mon 08:30-09:25, 1B Tue 09:30-10:25, 1C Thu 10:35-11:30
// Slot 2: 2A Mon 09:30-10:25, 2B Tue 10:35-11:30, 2C Thu 11:35-12:30
// Slot 3: 3A Mon 10:35-11:30, 3B Tue 11:35-12:30, 3C Thu 08:30-09:25
// Slot 4: 4A Mon 11:35-12:30, 4B Tue 08:30-09:25, 4C Thu 09:30-10:25

// EXTENDED SLOTS (5-6): 2 sub-slots, ~1.5h
// Slot 5: 5A Wed 09:30-10:55, 5B Fri 09:30-10:55
// Slot 6: 6A Wed 11:05-12:30, 6B Fri 11:05-12:30

// SHORT SLOT (7): 2 sub-slots, ~55 min
// Slot 7: 7A Wed 08:30-09:25, 7B Fri 08:30-09:25

// AFTERNOON SLOTS (8-11): 2 sub-slots, ~1.5h
// Slot 8:  8A Mon 14:00-15:25, 8B Thu 14:00-15:25
// Slot 9:  9A Mon 15:30-16:55, 9B Thu 15:30-16:55
// Slot 10: 10A Tue 14:00-15:25, 10B Fri 14:00-15:25
// Slot 11: 11A Tue 15:30-16:55, 11B Fri 15:30-16:55

// EVENING SLOTS (12-15): 2 sub-slots, ~1.5h
// Slot 12: 12A Mon 17:30-18:55, 12B Thu 17:30-18:55
// Slot 13: 13A Mon 19:00-20:25, 13B Thu 19:00-20:25
// Slot 14: 14A Tue 17:30-18:55, 14B Fri 17:30-18:55
// Slot 15: 15A Tue 19:00-20:25, 15B Fri 19:00-20:25

// LAB SLOTS (L1-L4): single 3h blocks
// L1: Mon 14:00-16:55, L2: Tue 14:00-16:55
// L3: Thu 14:00-16:55, L4: Fri 14:00-16:55

// WEDNESDAY EXTRA (X): special Wed afternoon/evening
// X1, X2, X3, Lx: Wed 14:00-16:55
// XC: Wed 17:30-18:55, XD: Wed 19:00-20:25
```

---

## Collection 4: `semesters`

```typescript
interface Semester extends AppwriteDocument {
  name: string          // "Semester 4", "Fall 2024"
  start_date: string    // ISO date "2026-01-15"
  end_date: string      // ISO date "2026-05-30"

  // APPEARANCE
  color: string         // hex "#8B5CF6" — drives accent color for whole page
  icon: string | null   // optional emoji or icon name

  // GOALS
  target_spi: number | null   // null = no goal set

  // RESULTS (for completed semesters)
  spi: number | null          // calculated or manual
  credits_earned: number | null
  credits_total: number | null
  is_quick_input: boolean     // true = user just entered SPI, no detailed tracking

  // STATUS
  status: 'upcoming' | 'ongoing' | 'completed'
  is_archived: boolean

  // AUDIT
  sort_order: number
  deleted_at: string | null
}

const createSemesterSchema = z.object({
  name: z.string().min(1, 'Name is required').max(100),
  start_date: z.string(),
  end_date: z.string(),
  color: z.string().regex(/^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$/, 'Invalid hex color'),
  icon: z.string().nullable().optional(),
  target_spi: z.number().min(0).nullable().optional(),
  is_quick_input: z.boolean().default(false),
  spi: z.number().min(0).nullable().optional(),
})

// RULES:
// - status is computed from dates: upcoming if start > today,
//   ongoing if start <= today <= end, completed if end < today
// - When semester is set to 'ongoing', set its color as the page accent color
// - deleted_at = soft delete (never hard delete semesters)
// - sort_order: higher = shown first (most recent at top)
```

---

## Collection 5: `subjects`

```typescript
interface Subject extends AppwriteDocument {
  semester_id: string    // FK to semesters.$id

  // BASIC INFO
  name: string           // "Data Structures & Algorithms"
  short_name: string     // "DSA" (shown in compact views, calendar)
  code: string | null    // "CS201" optional course code

  // DATES (override semester dates if different)
  start_date: string | null   // null = use semester start_date
  end_date: string | null     // null = use semester end_date

  // APPEARANCE
  color: string          // hex, defaults to semester color
  icon: string | null

  // ATTENDANCE
  attendance_requirement_percent: number | null  // null = use settings default (75%)

  // CREDITS + GRADING
  credits: number           // 0-100
  grade: string | null      // "A", "B+", etc. for completed subjects
  grade_points: number | null  // auto-calculated from grade OR manual
  grade_scale_id: string | null  // FK to grade_scales, null = use default

  // TYPE
  type: 'theory' | 'lab' | 'practical' | 'project' | 'other'

  // SLOT SYSTEM
  slot_ids: string[]  // e.g. ["1"] full Slot 1, or ["4B", "6A"] specific sub-slots
                      // empty array = manual scheduling (no slot)

  // TEACHER INFO (optional)
  teacher_name: string | null
  teacher_email: string | null
  teacher_phone: string | null

  // NOTIFICATIONS (per-subject override)
  telegram_notify_classes: boolean  // default: false

  // AUDIT
  sort_order: number
  deleted_at: string | null
}

const createSubjectSchema = z.object({
  semester_id: z.string().min(1),
  name: z.string().min(1).max(200),
  short_name: z.string().min(1).max(20),
  code: z.string().nullable().optional(),
  color: z.string().regex(/^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$/),
  attendance_requirement_percent: z.number().min(0).max(100).nullable().optional(),
  credits: z.number().min(0).max(100).default(0),
  type: z.enum(['theory', 'lab', 'practical', 'project', 'other']).default('theory'),
  slot_ids: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
  teacher_name: z.string().nullable().optional(),
  teacher_email: z.string().email().nullable().optional(),
})
```

---

## Collection 6: `class_schedules`

Defines WHEN a subject's classes repeat weekly.

```typescript
interface ClassSchedule extends AppwriteDocument {
  subject_id: string    // FK to subjects.$id

  // SLOT REFERENCE (if using slot system)
  slot_id: string | null      // e.g. "1" or null if manual
  sub_slot_id: string | null  // e.g. "1A" or null if manual

  // TIMING
  day_of_week: 1|2|3|4|5|6  // 1=Mon...6=Sat
  start_time: string          // "09:00"
  end_time: string            // "10:30"

  // LOCATION
  room: string | null
  building: string | null

  // VALIDITY (for mid-semester schedule changes)
  effective_from: string      // ISO date — when this schedule starts
  effective_until: string | null  // null = until semester ends

  // AUDIT
  deleted_at: string | null
}

// RULES:
// - When user picks a slot in Create Subject modal, auto-create class_schedules
//   (one per sub-slot chosen)
// - If user picks "full slot" (e.g. Slot 1), create 3 schedules (1A, 1B, 1C)
// - If mid-semester change: create NEW schedule with new effective_from,
//   set old one's effective_until to yesterday — preserves history
// - Never edit existing schedules — always create new ones for changes
```

---

## Collection 7: `class_occurrences`

Individual class sessions. NOT pre-generated for entire semester.
Created on-demand when user interacts with a class.

```typescript
interface ClassOccurrence extends AppwriteDocument {
  subject_id: string          // FK to subjects.$id
  schedule_id: string | null  // FK to class_schedules, null for extra classes

  // TIMING
  date: string        // ISO date "2026-03-28"
  start_time: string  // "09:00"
  end_time: string    // "10:30"

  // STATUS
  status: 'scheduled' | 'completed' | 'cancelled' | 'rescheduled'
  cancellation_reason: string | null
  rescheduled_to: string | null  // FK to another occurrence.$id

  // ATTENDANCE
  attendance: 'present' | 'absent' | null  // null = not yet marked
  attendance_marked_at: string | null       // ISO datetime
  attendance_note: string | null            // "late", "left early", etc.

  // TYPE
  is_extra_class: boolean  // true for one-off extra classes
}

// RULES:
// - NEVER pre-generate all occurrences for a semester (would be thousands)
// - Generate occurrences on-the-fly when rendering calendar view
// - Create occurrence record ONLY when user marks attendance or cancels a class
// - Auto-absent Appwrite Function creates occurrence with attendance='absent'
//   48 hours (settings.auto_absent_hours) after class was scheduled
// - UNIQUE constraint: (subject_id, date, start_time) — prevent duplicates
// - Cancelled classes do NOT count in attendance calculations
// - Extra class flow: is_extra_class=true, schedule_id=null
```

---

## Collection 8: `exams`

Tests, quizzes, midterms, finals, assignments.

```typescript
interface Exam extends AppwriteDocument {
  subject_id: string  // FK to subjects.$id

  // BASIC INFO
  name: string   // "Quiz 1", "Midterm", "Final Exam"
  type: 'quiz' | 'assignment' | 'midterm' | 'final' | 'practical' | 'other'

  // TIMING
  date: string           // ISO date
  start_time: string | null
  duration_minutes: number | null

  // LOCATION
  location: string | null  // "Hall A", "Lab 2"

  // MARKS
  marks_obtained: number | null   // null = not taken yet
  marks_total: number             // max marks
  weightage_percent: number | null  // contribution to final grade (0-100)

  // SYLLABUS
  syllabus: string | null   // "Chapters 1-5, Arrays, Linked Lists"
  notes: string | null      // personal notes about this exam

  // STATUS
  status: 'upcoming' | 'completed' | 'missed'

  // AUDIT
  deleted_at: string | null
}

const createExamSchema = z.object({
  subject_id: z.string().min(1),
  name: z.string().min(1).max(200),
  type: z.enum(['quiz', 'assignment', 'midterm', 'final', 'practical', 'other']),
  date: z.string(),
  start_time: z.string().nullable().optional(),
  duration_minutes: z.number().positive().nullable().optional(),
  marks_total: z.number().positive(),
  weightage_percent: z.number().min(0).max(100).nullable().optional(),
  syllabus: z.string().nullable().optional(),
})

// RULES:
// - Warn if total weightage for a subject exceeds 100%
// - status auto-computed: upcoming if date > today, completed if marks_obtained set
// - marks_obtained can be set after exam date passes
```

---

## Collection 9: `tasks`

To-dos with optional deadlines. Can be linked to a subject or standalone.

```typescript
interface Task extends AppwriteDocument {
  subject_id: string | null   // optional — null = personal task
  semester_id: string | null  // optional — for context

  // CONTENT
  title: string
  description: string | null
  notes: string | null

  // DEADLINE
  deadline: string | null     // ISO datetime
  reminder_at: string | null  // ISO datetime for notification

  // STATUS
  is_completed: boolean
  completed_at: string | null  // ISO datetime

  // PRIORITY
  priority: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' | null

  // AUDIT
  sort_order: number
  deleted_at: string | null
}

const createTaskSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string().min(1).max(500),
  description: z.string().nullable().optional(),
  subject_id: z.string().nullable().optional(),
  semester_id: z.string().nullable().optional(),
  deadline: z.string().nullable().optional(),
  priority: z.enum(['high', 'medium', 'low']).nullable().optional(),
})

// RULES:
// - Completing a task: set is_completed=true, completed_at=now
// - 5-second undo window after completion (use Sonner toast with undo action)
// - Swipe right on mobile to complete
// - Overdue = deadline < now AND NOT completed
```

---

## Collection 10: `events`

Personal calendar events (birthdays, meetings, appointments, etc.)

```typescript
interface Event extends AppwriteDocument {
  semester_id: string | null  // optional — show on semester calendar if set

  // CONTENT
  title: string
  description: string | null
  location: string | null

  // TIMING
  start_datetime: string   // ISO datetime
  end_datetime: string     // ISO datetime
  is_all_day: boolean

  // RECURRENCE (basic)
  recurrence: 'none' | 'daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly' | null

  // APPEARANCE
  color: string | null  // null = use accent color

  // AUDIT
  deleted_at: string | null
}
```

---

## Collection 11: `holidays`

Days that cancel ALL classes automatically.

```typescript
interface Holiday extends AppwriteDocument {
  // BASIC INFO
  name: string          // "Diwali", "Republic Day", "Snow Day"
  date: string          // ISO date
  date_end: string | null  // null = single day, set for multi-day holidays

  // BEHAVIOR
  cancels_all_classes: boolean  // always true for holidays
  description: string | null

  // AUDIT
  deleted_at: string | null
}

// RULES:
// - When a holiday is added, all class_occurrences on that date are
//   automatically set to status='cancelled', cancellation_reason='Holiday: {name}'
// - Show on calendar as full-day colored block
// - Cancelled holiday classes do NOT count toward attendance
```

---

## Collection 12: `resource_links`

YouTube, Notion, Drive, and other links per subject.

```typescript
interface ResourceLink extends AppwriteDocument {
  subject_id: string   // FK to subjects.$id
  exam_id: string | null  // optional — link to specific exam

  // CONTENT
  title: string
  url: string
  type: 'youtube' | 'notion' | 'drive' | 'github' | 'website' | 'other'
  description: string | null
  thumbnail_url: string | null  // auto-fetched for YouTube

  // AUDIT
  sort_order: number
  deleted_at: string | null
}
```

---

## Collection 13: `files`

File metadata. Actual files stored in Appwrite Storage bucket.

```typescript
interface ClasseyFile extends AppwriteDocument {
  // STORAGE
  storage_file_id: string   // Appwrite Storage file ID
  file_name: string         // original filename
  file_size: number         // bytes
  mime_type: string
  file_extension: string    // 'pdf', 'py', 'cpp', 'xlsx', etc.

  // CONTEXT LINKS (file can be linked to multiple entities)
  subject_id: string | null
  exam_id: string | null
  task_id: string | null
  event_id: string | null
  occurrence_id: string | null  // specific class session

  // METADATA
  description: string | null
  is_past_paper: boolean  // true for past exam papers

  // AUDIT
  deleted_at: string | null
}

// ALLOWED FILE TYPES:
// Documents: pdf, txt, doc, docx, ppt, pptx, xls, xlsx
// Code: py, js, ts, c, cpp, h, java
// Images: jpg, jpeg, png, gif, webp, svg
// Max file size: determined by Appwrite Storage settings

// RULES:
// - Files are contextual — not just stored, they're linked to specific content
// - Smart file linking: link a PDF to a specific lecture occurrence, exam, or task
// - is_past_paper: flag for past exam papers, shown separately in subject Files tab
```

---

## Collection 14: `notifications_log`

Track sent notifications to avoid duplicates.

```typescript
interface NotificationLog extends AppwriteDocument {
  // REFERENCE
  entity_type: 'exam' | 'task' | 'class' | 'deadline' | 'assignment'
  entity_id: string       // FK to the relevant collection

  // DELIVERY
  channel: 'telegram' | 'push'
  sent_at: string         // ISO datetime
  message_preview: string // first 100 chars of message
  success: boolean
  error_message: string | null
}
```

---

## Collection 15: `audit_log`

Full change history for all entities.

```typescript
interface AuditLog extends AppwriteDocument {
  // WHAT CHANGED
  entity_type: string   // "semester", "subject", "exam", "task", etc.
  entity_id: string
  action: 'create' | 'update' | 'delete' | 'restore'

  // CHANGE DETAILS
  changes: string   // JSON stringified { field: { from, to } }
  changed_at: string  // ISO datetime
}
```

---

## Collection 16: `tags`

Universal tagging system.

```typescript
interface Tag extends AppwriteDocument {
  name: string    // "important", "revision", "assignment"
  color: string   // hex color for tag badge
}
```

---

## Collection 17: `tagged_items`

Assigns tags to any entity.

```typescript
interface TaggedItem extends AppwriteDocument {
  tag_id: string        // FK to tags.$id
  entity_type: string   // "exam", "task", "file", "subject", etc.
  entity_id: string     // FK to the entity
}
```

---

## Collection 18: `study_sessions`

Pomodoro tracking. Schema ready for future implementation.

```typescript
interface StudySession extends AppwriteDocument {
  subject_id: string | null
  task_id: string | null
  started_at: string   // ISO datetime
  ended_at: string | null
  duration_minutes: number | null
  session_type: 'pomodoro' | 'free' | null
  notes: string | null
}
```

---

## Collection 19: `goals`

Flexible goal tracking across subjects and semesters.

```typescript
interface Goal extends AppwriteDocument {
  semester_id: string | null
  subject_id: string | null

  title: string
  goal_type: 'attendance' | 'spi' | 'grade' | 'tasks' | 'custom'
  target_value: number
  current_value: number
  unit: string   // "%", "SPI points", "tasks", etc.

  deadline: string | null
  is_achieved: boolean
  achieved_at: string | null
}
```

---

## Collection 20: `notes`

Quick notes attached to class sessions or subjects.

```typescript
interface Note extends AppwriteDocument {
  subject_id: string
  occurrence_id: string | null  // null = general subject note

  content: string
  is_pinned: boolean
}
```

---

## Collection 21: `backups`

Tracks export/backup history.

```typescript
interface Backup extends AppwriteDocument {
  export_type: 'json' | 'csv'
  file_name: string
  file_size: number | null   // bytes
  exported_at: string        // ISO datetime
  status: 'success' | 'failed'
  includes: string           // JSON: which collections were included
}
```

---

## Collection 22: `widgets_config`

Dashboard widget layout and settings.

```typescript
interface WidgetConfig extends AppwriteDocument {
  widget_id: string    // "attendance-ring", "upcoming-exams", "task-list", etc.
  is_visible: boolean
  position: number     // order in dashboard
  config: string | null  // JSON stringified widget-specific settings
}
```

---

## Collection 23: `ai_conversations`

Last 10–20 AI chat messages. Auto-pruned.

```typescript
interface AiConversation extends AppwriteDocument {
  role: 'user' | 'assistant'
  content: string
  timestamp: string   // ISO datetime
  tokens_used: number | null
  provider: 'groq' | 'google' | null
}

// RULES:
// - Max 20 messages stored at any time
// - When adding new message: if count >= 20, delete oldest before inserting
// - Query ordered by timestamp ASC for chat display
// - Query ordered by timestamp DESC to find oldest for deletion
// - Include last N messages as context when calling AI API
```

---

## TanStack Query Patterns

```typescript
// src/hooks/use-semesters.ts — example pattern for ALL hooks

import { useQuery, useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'
import { databases, DB_ID, COLLECTIONS } from '@/lib/appwrite'
import { Query } from 'appwrite'

// Query keys — centralize all query keys
export const semesterKeys = {
  all: ['semesters'] as const,
  lists: () => [...semesterKeys.all, 'list'] as const,
  detail: (id: string) => [...semesterKeys.all, 'detail', id] as const,
}

// Fetch all semesters
export function useSemesters() {
  return useQuery({
    queryKey: semesterKeys.lists(),
    queryFn: async () => {
      const response = await databases.listDocuments(
        DB_ID,
        COLLECTIONS.SEMESTERS,
        [
          Query.isNull('deleted_at'),
          Query.orderDesc('sort_order'),
        ]
      )
      return response.documents as Semester[]
    },
  })
}

// Create semester — with optimistic update
export function useCreateSemester() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
  return useMutation({
    mutationFn: async (data: CreateSemesterInput) => {
      return databases.createDocument(DB_ID, COLLECTIONS.SEMESTERS,
        ID.unique(), data)
    },
    onSuccess: () => {
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: semesterKeys.lists() })
    },
  })
}

// Delete semester — soft delete + optimistic update + undo
export function useDeleteSemester() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
  return useMutation({
    mutationFn: async (id: string) => {
      return databases.updateDocument(DB_ID, COLLECTIONS.SEMESTERS, id, {
        deleted_at: new Date().toISOString()
      })
    },
    onMutate: async (id) => {
      // Optimistic update
      await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: semesterKeys.lists() })
      const previous = queryClient.getQueryData(semesterKeys.lists())
      queryClient.setQueryData(semesterKeys.lists(), (old: Semester[]) =>
        old?.filter(s => s.$id !== id)
      )
      return { previous }
    },
    onError: (_, __, context) => {
      // Rollback on error
      queryClient.setQueryData(semesterKeys.lists(), context?.previous)
    },
    onSettled: () => {
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: semesterKeys.lists() })
    },
  })
}
```

---

## Appwrite Query Reference

```typescript
import { Query } from 'appwrite'

// Common queries used in Classey
Query.equal('semester_id', semesterId)
Query.equal('subject_id', subjectId)
Query.isNull('deleted_at')             // soft delete filter — ALWAYS include
Query.orderDesc('$createdAt')
Query.orderAsc('sort_order')
Query.greaterThanEqual('date', today)  // upcoming items
Query.lessThan('date', today)          // past items
Query.equal('status', 'ongoing')
Query.equal('is_completed', false)
Query.isNull('attendance')             // unmarked classes
Query.limit(20)
Query.offset(0)

// ALWAYS filter soft-deleted records
// EVERY query to any collection must include: Query.isNull('deleted_at')
// Exception: audit_log and notifications_log (never soft-deleted)
```

---

## Appwrite Storage — File Operations

```typescript
// Upload file
const file = await storage.createFile(BUCKET_ID, ID.unique(), fileInput)

// Get file preview URL (images)
const previewUrl = storage.getFilePreview(BUCKET_ID, fileId, 300, 300)

// Get file download URL
const downloadUrl = storage.getFileDownload(BUCKET_ID, fileId)

// Delete file
await storage.deleteFile(BUCKET_ID, fileId)

// RULES:
// - Always create Files collection document AFTER successful storage upload
// - If Files document creation fails, delete the uploaded file (cleanup)
// - Use storage.getFilePreview for thumbnails in file browser
// - Use storage.getFileDownload for actual file access
```
